Class OrderServiceImpl

java.lang.Object
com.wealthpro.orderexecution.service.OrderServiceImpl
All Implemented Interfaces:
OrderService
@Service @Transactional public class OrderServiceImpl extends Object implements OrderService
Implementation of OrderService containing the core business logic for the complete order lifecycle — from placement through pre-trade validation, routing, execution, allocation, and cancellation.
Version:
2.0
Author:
WealthPro Team

      Update an existing order's details.

    • deleteOrder

      @CacheEvict(value="orders", key="#orderId") public void deleteOrder(Long orderId)
      Description copied from interface: OrderService
      Delete an order by ID.
      Specified by:
      deleteOrder in interface OrderService

    • runAllPreTradeChecks

      @CacheEvict(value="orders", key="#orderId") public List<PreTradeCheckResponseDTO> runAllPreTradeChecks(Long orderId)
      Run all four pre-trade compliance checks (SUITABILITY, LIMIT, EXPOSURE, CASH) against the specified order. Auto-transitions the order to VALIDATED if all pass, or REJECTED if any fail.

      Runs all four simulated pre-trade checks (SUITABILITY, LIMIT, EXPOSURE, CASH). If every check passes, the order status is set to VALIDATED. If any check fails, the order status is set to REJECTED.

      Specified by:
      runAllPreTradeChecks in interface OrderService
      Parameters:
      orderId - the order to validate
      Returns:
      list of check results

    • routeOrder

      @CacheEvict(value="orders", key="#orderId") public OrderResponseDTO routeOrder(Long orderId)
      Route a VALIDATED order to a simulated exchange venue. Transitions the order to ROUTED.

      Simulates routing by assigning a dummy venue name.

      Specified by:
      routeOrder in interface OrderService
      Parameters:
      orderId - the order to route
      Returns:
      updated order with routedVenue and ROUTED status

    • executeFill

      @CacheEvict(value="orders", key="#orderId") public ExecutionFillResponseDTO executeFill(Long orderId, ExecutionFillRequestDTO requestDTO)
      Record an execution fill and auto-update order status (PARTIALLY_FILLED or FILLED based on cumulative fill quantity).

      Creates a fill and calculates the cumulative filled quantity to determine whether the order should move to PARTIALLY_FILLED or FILLED.

      Specified by:
      executeFill in interface OrderService
      Parameters:
      orderId - the order being filled
      requestDTO - fill details
      Returns:
      the recorded fill

    • allocateOrder

      public AllocationResponseDTO allocateOrder(Long orderId, AllocationRequestDTO requestDTO)
      Allocate a FILLED order to a client account.

      Only FILLED orders can have allocations created.

      Specified by:
      allocateOrder in interface OrderService
      Parameters:
      orderId - the filled order
      requestDTO - allocation details
      Returns:
      the recorded allocation

    • cancelOrder

      @CacheEvict(value="orders", key="#orderId") public OrderResponseDTO cancelOrder(Long orderId)
      Cancel an order. Only orders in PLACED, VALIDATED, or ROUTED status can be cancelled.

      Cancellation is only allowed for PLACED, VALIDATED, or ROUTED orders.

      Specified by:
      cancelOrder in interface OrderService
      Parameters:
      orderId - the order to cancel
      Returns:
      updated order with CANCELLED status

    • getOrderLifecycle

      @Transactional(readOnly=true) public OrderLifecycleDTO getOrderLifecycle(Long orderId)
      Retrieve the full lifecycle view of an order — the order itself plus all related pre-trade checks, execution fills, and allocations.

      Assembles the full lifecycle view by querying all related entities.

      Specified by:
      getOrderLifecycle in interface OrderService
      Parameters:
      orderId - the order ID
      Returns:
      comprehensive lifecycle DTO
